Fine to proceed toward tomorrow's lock with this included in the May close package.\", \"user\": \"npc_016\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780412880.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_028_monthly_close_reviewer_approves_entries_and_lock_path_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780507920.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"From tax, the May revaluation piece reads as a non-cash translation gain only. I do not see a federal deduction-timing implication from the GBP payment timing here, so this should stay a book treatment question rather than a tax timing item.\", \"user\": \"persona_004\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_019_orphan_exception_peer_and_tax_breadcrumbs_exist_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780507800.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"JE shape looks right from the prepaid schedule side: DR Prepaid Facilities Maintenance 435.41 and CR FX Translation Gain 435.41. If we book it in FP-2026-06, the prepaid amortization schedule will pick up the revised carry value automatically on the next monthly run, so no manual catch-up line needed.\", \"user\": \"persona_014\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780497720.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_019_orphan_exception_peer_and_tax_breadcrumbs_exist_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780500240.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I pulled the AP autoroute history after your question. Datadog has been default-routing into 500000 across FY25, and the cumulative misclassification is roughly $340K. Presentation-wise that is material to gross margin, but it is immaterial to net income since it is all within opex/COR classification. I'm drafting a SAB 99 quant memo for Andrea so the close package can support a go-forward-only treatment unless she wants a broader retrospective cleanup.\", \"user\": \"persona_009\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780412880.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_028_monthly_close_preparer_quantifies_misclassification_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780498440.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Thanks for catching this. Do we know whether Datadog has been hitting 500000 for just May or for a few prior months as well? If it has been miscoded for a while, I want to understand whether we need a prior-period adjustment or if a go-forward correction in May is sufficient. Mainly trying to gauge whether the gross margin presentation issue is material enough to require more than a cleanup this month.\", \"user\": \"npc_016\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780412880.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_028_monthly_close_reviewer_questions_prior_period_scope_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780497720.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Need same-day approval help on BlackLine exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c tied to recon BL-3978BDB68290 for account 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance. James Randall is the approver on it and the SLA is June 4. Jones and I landed on a corrective JE in FP-2026-06 rather than treating the $435.41 as translation noise. Ben Arinzo, can you sanity-check the prepaid amortization schedule shape against the proposed JE? Hannah Grant, can you weigh in on the GBP payment timing tax angle from the February 2026 payment on the London corporate-services prepaid block? Want to get this cleared today.\", \"user\": \"persona_003\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1780507800.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_019_orphan_exception_slack_thread_request_exists_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780425480.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Yes  -  the underlying outside-counsel work is all May activity. The timesheets were cut off through 5/29, and the only thing lagging into June is the vendor invoicing, not the service period. On that basis the $52,185 accrual is fully deductible in FY26. I'll add a one-line note to the management accounts package commentary so the treatment and reasoning are on the record.\", \"user\": \"persona_001\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780327320.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_024_monthly_close_preparer_answers_and_commits_note_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780423860.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"George, one partner question before we let this sit: were the outside-counsel services underlying the $52,185 accrual actually rendered in May, or does any portion slide into June? Please answer it explicitly in matching-principle terms so we are not deferring expense that belongs in May if the work was performed before month-end.\", \"user\": \"persona_008\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780327320.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_024_monthly_close_partner_asks_deductibility_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780422840.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Reviewed the three May entries for brookfield_FP-2026-05. Accrual increase vs April tracks with the Q2 wrap timing Andrea flagged last month; CPE and depreciation are flat as expected. I'm comfortable taking this file into tomorrow's lock cycle.\", \"user\": \"npc_020\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780327320.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_024_monthly_close_reviewer_greenlight_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780412880.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"All three Acme BD1 entries are through. Deferred revenue release posted at $194.8K, commissions amortization posted at $74.2K, and the $28.4K Datadog reclass is in from 500000 Cost of Revenue - Cloud Infrastructure to 521000 Engineering Tools & Observability. I caught the Datadog coding issue while cross-checking AP vouchers against the commissions roll. One open item still sitting in the May close pool: Emily Adekole's Cash - Tax Reserve reconciliation BL-D603C8DB90D7.\", \"user\": \"persona_009\", \"reply_count\": 3, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1780508520.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_028_monthly_close_announce_bd1_status_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780327320.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"BD1 close entries are posted for brookfield_FP-2026-05. Accrual landed at $52,185 as the Q2 wrap is still running; CPE and depreciation are flat to prior cadence. Anaya's payroll tax reconciliation BL-32EDC5791080 is still in progress, but it does not affect these JEs. Handing the package over to Margaret for review.\", \"user\": \"persona_001\", \"reply_count\": 3, \"reply_users_count\": 3, \"latest_reply\": \"1780425480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_024_monthly_close_handoff_review_status_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780324380.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I checked the May WIP feed area in Records Vault this morning. It only shows the same partial-success support Anaya already pointed to, Brookfield May 2026 WIP engagement-hours rollforward, and I do not see any orphaned source document, alternate title, or metadata stub that ties to the remaining $4,390.62 on BL-75810CD0FEE4 / account 119000. That probably closes out the vault breadcrumb from my side, so accept-timing for FP-2026-05 looks like the likely path unless Hannah wants us to escalate further.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248600.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_010_orphan_exception_edith_reports_no_matching_doc_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780323420.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Quick thread update: Daniel Jones is OK with locking disposition as accept-timing for exc_aade06f6129e43. We are keeping the exception in logged state until the June 2 BD2 retry runs, and I added a management-accounts commentary line so the narrative path is documented without writing anything into BlackLine or other accounting systems yet.\", \"user\": \"persona_013\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780147500.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_009_orphan_exception_follow_up_reminder_and_thread_update_exist_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780320720.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Partner view: I support the corrective-JE path. Accept-timing is the wrong call here because that $172 stub would sit on the May deferred-revenue reconciliation and show up in my weekly partner revenue review. If the work was earned in May and the billing landed after BD3, cleanest answer is to true up the May/June split in June rather than wave it through.\", \"user\": \"persona_008\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_011_orphan_exception_reviewer_and_partner_inputs_exist_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780320600.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"From a rev-rec/tax lens, retainer revenue is recognized when earned, not when cash was originally deferred. If the late client billing means May services were earned before BD3 but the release did not get recorded until June, a corrective JE in FP-2026-06 fixes the inter-period split without changing the FY-2026 total. Only caveat: please confirm the earned-versus-deferred split if that invoice spans two quarters.\", \"user\": \"persona_004\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248300.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_011_orphan_exception_reviewer_and_partner_inputs_exist_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780254120.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"@Edith Banda could you do a quick records-vault metadata sweep for anything tied to the May WIP subledger feed batch? Anaya only remembers Brookfield May 2026 WIP engagement-hours rollforward. If an orphaned source document exists for the missing $4,390.62 detail on account 119000 / BL-75810CD0FEE4, title, vault ID, or even just document-kind references would be enough.\", \"user\": \"persona_001\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248600.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_010_orphan_exception_george_loops_edith_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780253280.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"We had the same shape on 240000 in FP-2025-09: there was a $310 unrecorded invoice sitting in deferred revenue at month-end, and we did not accept-timing it. We pushed a corrective JE in FP-2025-10 because the deferred-revenue release was feeding the quarter's revenue KPI, so leaving the stub would have distorted the metric. Same logic applies to this May-to-June handover  -  small dollars, but not a timing waive on a KPI-sensitive rev-rec balance.\", \"user\": \"persona_001\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248300.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_011_orphan_exception_peer_precedent_visible_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780251900.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"From close-team side this is not blocking, and at $9.61 I don't see anything that would affect any individual partner allocation in a meaningful way. Still, Tom should run the standard approval flow so Daniel's signed disposition is the audit-trail breadcrumb if exc_652c0931bb2546 ever gets sampled later.\", \"user\": \"persona_028\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780242120.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_012_orphan_exception_reshma_procedure_note_exists_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780251480.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I saw the same shape on account 133000 in FP-2026-03. We had a partial batch leave an unsupported residual, then it cleared in the next period after the feed reran. This is the key thread. In **C005, thread 1780147500** (exc_aade06f6129e43 on BL-333FF9956BC6):
- **persona_013** opened it: "$-28.59 feed-drop tied to **29 dropped rows** on 102000... accept timing pending the **June BD2 feed retry Ryan flagged**"
- **persona_001 (George)**: cites **FP-2025-11 precedent** ($42 feed-drop residual, retry cleared it)
- **persona_004 (Hannah)**: tax — payroll-withholding impact immaterial
- **persona_013** later: "**Daniel Jones is OK with accept-timing**... keeping in logged state until June 2 BD2 retry"

Meanwhile my own recon says "FX revaluation." Let me pull the exception, the feeds, GL activity, and identities.

The precedent **does not hold**. content.text: The Blue↔Ryan messaging confirms the origin: **Ryan Delgado** authored the recommendation — "June retry is already queued for BD2, my recommendation is accept-timing... loop in Hannah and George" — and Blue pushed back ("should we do something immediately"). Daniel Jones (Accounts Manager) signed it off. Let me pull the rest of the directory to identify persona_013 (the close-thread coordinator), then I'll have the full cast.

content.text:
I now have the full cast and the evidence. Let me record the key findings:

**The accept-timing story (3 claims) vs. the systems:**
- **Ryan Delgado** (Audit Senior) originated it in messaging to Blue + raised exception `exc_aade06f6129e43` as "subledger_feed_drop / 29 dropped rows," recommended accept-timing pending "June BD2 retry."
- **persona_013** formalized it in the C005 close thread, logged sign-off.
- **Daniel Jones** (Accounts Manager) signed off accept-timing.
- **George McAdam** cited an FP-2025-11 precedent.
- My own recon line says "FX revaluation" — a *third* different cause.

**What the systems actually show:** GL 102000 had **zero** May activity; **both** feeds posting to it ran 100% clean (Payroll 330/330, Treasury 2616/2616, 0 rejected); precedent was -3.42 sub-threshold with **no** feed-drop note; no FX reval JE. Nothing dropped, nothing to retry.

Let me record findings in BlackLine (correct my own unsupported line + add a review note), set Ben's follow-up reminder, and email the recommendation/sign-off chain.

input.subject: FP-2026-05 Cash-Payroll (102000) variance — accept-timing not supported by the systems
input.content:
Hi all,

Blue asked me to sanity-check the cause on the small -28.59 Cash-Payroll variance (102000) for brookfield_FP-2026-05 / recon BL-333FF9956BC6 / exception exc_aade06f6129e43 before it's locked as accept-timing. I post the payroll cash entries on that account, so I went to the source systems. The accept-timing story does not hold up. Laying it out plainly against each claim:

1) CLAIMED FEED DROP ("subledger feed dropped 29 rows that fed 102000")
Not supported. GL account 102000 had ZERO posted activity in FP-2026-05 — opening = closing = 2,192,357.22, debits 0, credits 0, and no journal-entry line in the whole period references 102000. I also ran the full period feed history for every feed that posts to that account, not just one:
  - Payroll feed (brookfield_payroll_feed) run_1fb45b81237648: 330 in / 330 posted / 0 rejected — success.
  - Treasury & Bank Activity feed (brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed) run_ad0acbb9675443: 2,616 in / 2,616 posted / 0 rejected — success.
No feed dropped any rows into this account. If 29 rows had genuinely dropped, they'd show as rejected rows on a run and as missing GL postings — neither exists.

2) JUNE BD2 RETRY ("the June retry will clear it")
There is nothing to retry. Both feeds completed 100% for the period with zero rejects; there is no failed or partial batch queued. A retry has no dropped rows to pick up, so this variance will not "clear" on its own.

3) PRIOR-PERIOD PRECEDENT (FP-2025-11, cited as ~$42 feed-drop residual accepted as timing and cleared by retry)
The precedent doesn't match. The actual brookfield FP-2025-11 102000 recon is BL-8DCA6908E272: variance was -3.42 (not ~$42), it was UNDER the $10 threshold, and it was certified with NO variance explanation at all — there is no recorded "feed-drop accepted as timing, cleared by retry" event on this account. (The FP-2025-11 payroll feed run also posted 119/119, 0 rejected.) For context, GL 102000 has sat unchanged at 2,192,357.22 every month since FP-2025-07 — it's a dormant funding account with no feed activity, so a "rows dropped that fed the account" narrative doesn't fit it.

Also for the record: the recon previously carried an "FX revaluation rates refreshed" note in my name. That is equally unsupported (no revaluation JE posted to 102000) — I've corrected it.

WHAT THE EVIDENCE ACTUALLY SUPPORTS
The -28.59 is a supporting-schedule reconciling difference with no GL counterpart (supporting 2,192,385.81 vs a GL that didn't move). It needs to be tied back to the supporting source and resolved, not waved through as feed-drop timing. Please don't lock exc_aade06f6129e43 as accept-timing on the current basis. I've recorded the source-system findings on BL-333FF9956BC6 (corrected variance note + review note RN-680F9FE1) so the artifact reflects this before any submit/certify.

Happy to walk through the feed runs or GL detail with anyone.

Thanks,
Ben

input.payload:
Flagging before this locks as accept-timing — I post the payroll cash entries on 102000 and checked the source systems. The feed-drop read doesn't hold:

• Claimed feed drop (29 rows): not supported. GL 102000 had ZERO posted activity in FP-2026-05 (opening=closing=2,192,357.22; 0 debits/0 credits; no JE lines hit it). Every feed that posts to the account ran clean for the period — Payroll run_1fb45b81237648 330/330 (0 rejected), Treasury run_ad0acbb9675443 2,616/2,616 (0 rejected). No dropped rows anywhere.
• June BD2 retry: nothing to retry — both feeds posted 100%, no failed/partial batch is queued, so this won't self-clear.
• @george.mcadam the FP-2025-11 precedent doesn't match: that recon (BL-8DCA6908E272) was -3.42, under threshold, certified with no feed-drop explanation. 102000 has been flat at 2,192,357.22 since FP-2025-07 — a dormant account with no feed activity.

@ryan.delgado @daniel.jones net: the -28.59 is a supporting-side reconciling difference with no GL counterpart, not a feed drop and not FX reval. It needs tie-out to source, not accept-timing. I've corrected the variance note on BL-333FF9956BC6 and added review note RN-680F9FE1 with the detail.

Can we hold the accept-timing disposition until it's tied out?

content.text:
Done. Here's what I found and what I did with it.

## What the thread is claiming (and who's claiming it)
This variance (-$28.59 on **102000 Cash-Payroll**, FP-2026-05, recon **BL-333FF9956BC6**, exception **exc_aade06f6129e43**) has been discussed in several places, and the stories don't line up:

- **Ryan Delgado** (Audit Senior) — *origin of the recommendation.* Raised the exception as type "subledger_feed_drop / **29 dropped rows**," and in a DM to Blue (29 May) said *"June retry is already queued for BD2… my recommendation is accept-timing"* and to loop in Hannah + George.
- **persona_013** (close coordinator) — opened the C005 triage thread, proposed accept-timing, and logged that *"Daniel Jones is OK with accept-timing… keep in logged state until the June 2 BD2 retry."*
- **Daniel Jones** (Accounts Manager) — the sign-off.
- **George McAdam** — supplied the *"FP-2025-11 precedent — ~$42 feed-drop residual accepted as timing, cleared by retry."*
- **Hannah Grant** — tax: payroll-withholding impact immaterial (a side point, not the cause).
- **The recon itself** carried a *fourth* story in your name: *"FX revaluation rates refreshed."*

## What the systems actually show — the accept-timing read falls apart
1. **Claimed feed drop: not real.** GL 102000 had **zero posted activity** in FP-2026-05 (opening = closing = 2,192,357.22; 0 debits/0 credits; **no JE line** references 102000 in the whole period). I ran the **full period feed history for every feed that posts to the account**: Payroll feed `run_1fb45b81237648` = 330/330 posted, **0 rejected**; Treasury feed `run_ad0acbb9675443` = 2,616/2,616 posted, **0 rejected**. No 29 dropped rows anywhere.
2. **June BD2 retry: nothing to retry.** Both feeds completed 100% with no failed/partial batch — there's no dropped batch for a retry to pick up, so it won't self-clear.
3. **Precedent doesn't hold.** The real FP-2025-11 recon (**BL-8DCA6908E272**) was **-3.42** (not ~$42), **under threshold**, certified with **no feed-drop explanation**. 102000 has been frozen at 2,192,357.22 every month since FP-2025-07 — a dormant account with no feed activity.
4. **The "FX revaluation" note was also unsupported** (no revaluation JE posted).

**Real cause:** the -$28.59 is a **supporting-schedule reconciling difference with no GL counterpart** (supporting 2,192,385.81 vs a GL that hasn't moved). It needs **tie-out to source**, not accept-timing.

## Who I squared it with
- **BlackLine recon BL-333FF9956BC6:** replaced the incorrect "FX revaluation" variance note with the evidenced finding, and added review note **RN-680F9FE1** — so the artifact reflects reality before any submit/certify.
- **Email** to **Ryan Delgado, Daniel Jones, George McAdam, Blue Evans** (cc **Hannah Grant**) laying out the feed-drop, June-retry, and precedent claims each addressed.
- **Close thread (C005, the triage thread):** posted the same evidence in-thread. ⚠️ Note: the Slack client posted it under **George McAdam's** handle (it wouldn't accept your user-id), so I added a follow-up line clarifying the analysis is yours, posted on your behalf, and that the email is the authoritative copy.
- **Direct messages** (correctly from you) to **Ryan Delgado** (where he raised it) and **Blue Evans** (who asked).
- **Reminder set** for 13 Jun 08:30 to chase before the period locks/certifies.

One flag for you: the Slack misattribution is the only loose end — if you want it cleanly under your name, you may want to repost from your own Slack session, since the tool defaulted the sender to George.
